inscriptions.text: PEAKE-FAIRFAX HOUSE  KNOWN AS “THE HOUSE WITH TWO DOORS” OWING TO ITS UNIQUE DUAL ENTRANCES, THIS IS THE CHILDHOOD HOME OF CELEBRATED CIVIL WAR DIARIST CORNELIA PEAKE McDONALD (1822~1909), DAUGHTER OF DR. WILLIAM HUMPHREY PEAKE III, COLLECTOR OF THE PORT OF ALEXANDRIA UNDER PRESIDENTS MONROE AND JOHN QUINCY ADAMS. IN 1831, GENERAL ORLANDO FAIRFAX, MD, SON OF LORD THOMAS FAIRFAX, ESTABLISHED HIS MEDICAL OFFICES HERE. BOTH THE PEAKE AND FAIRFAX FAMILIES WERE CLOSE FRIENDS WITH GEORGE AND MARTHA WASHINGTON.  PLACED BY THE COLONIAL DAMES OF AMERICA
